description: Executable evals for the glossary skill (per docs/superpowers/decisions/2026-05-15-glossary-skill.md §4 + post-review fixes for PR #320). Each eval shells `claude --print`, captures the response, and runs rubric assertions against it. Coverage: lazy ./CONTEXT.md creation on first term (with structural Write anchor), _Avoid_ alias recording, format compliance with grill-with-docs CONTEXT-FORMAT.md, DTP/SA caller-hook offer-on-resolution and skip-on-no-resolution, decline-does-not-write (Bash escape closed), Flagged-ambiguity surfacing on conflicting alias, plus skip-all reply branch, alphabetical-order append, --alias-of <missing-canonical> subflow, no-conflict-no-flag idempotency twin, --list and --conflict read-only surfaces. Per memory note `feedback_sunk_cost_eval.md`: substrate-fixable failures stay at the eval-runner layer; do NOT chase them with skill or rules edits. Tier annotations: structural assertions (skill_invoked, tool_called, tool_input_matches, not_tool_called) are tier:required and gate CI; loose prose-channel regexes are tier:diagnostic and report only.
_contract_note: Required-tier `tool_input_matches` and `skill_invoked` assertions pin the Claude Code Skill tool surface (tool name 'Skill', input_key 'skill', input_value 'glossary') AND the Write/Bash tool surfaces. These are unversioned upstream contracts owned by Claude Code. If an upstream rename lands, every required-tier assertion silently flips red without signaling platform change vs. regression. Confirm tool schemas before treating a required-tier failure as a behavioral regression.
_substrate_note: Several evals (lazy-create-on-first-term, format-matches-grill-context-format, append-to-existing-alphabetical-order, alias-of-missing-canonical) require `claude --print` to actually write files in its scratch cwd. If those evals fail with no Write tool use observed, suspect substrate (sandbox forbids writes, scratch dir read-only, etc.) before suspecting the skill body — this is the failure class memory note feedback_sunk_cost_eval.md warns about. Triage path: re-run with EVAL_TEXT_NONBLOCKING=1 to demote text-channel failures, then check whether tool_called Write fires across the suite at all.

evals.name: lazy-create-on-first-term
evals.summary: First /glossary invocation in a project with no existing ./CONTEXT.md must lazy-create the file with the grill-with-docs format (heading + Language section). Structural Write anchor closes the prose-fake-pass failure mode.
evals.prompt:
/glossary Customer

This project has no ./CONTEXT.md yet. Project context name: "Reflex Ordering". Description: "Order intake and fulfillment for the Reflex retail platform." Definition of Customer: "A person or organization that places orders." Aliases to avoid: client, buyer, account. No relationships to record.

assertions.description: /glossary slash command invokes the glossary skill (structural)
Write tool fires — file actually created on disk, not just described in prose
Write target ends in CONTEXT.md (lazy-create at repo root)
File body contains the project-context heading (prose-channel — Write content is not surfaced in finalText, so this is best-effort against summary)
File body or summary references ## Language section header
Customer mentioned as a bolded canonical term
Aliases recorded under _Avoid_:

evals.name: alias-of-recorded-under-canonical
evals.summary: /glossary <alias> --alias-of <canonical> records the alias on the _Avoid_: line under the canonical term, not as a separate entry.
evals.prompt:
Project ./CONTEXT.md already exists with this content:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment for the Reflex retail platform.

## Language

**Customer**:
A person or organization that places orders.
_Avoid_:
```

Now run: /glossary account --alias-of Customer

assertions.description: /glossary invokes glossary skill (structural)
account appears on the _Avoid_: line
account is NOT introduced as a separate canonical entry (any line with **account**: as a heading)

evals.name: format-matches-grill-context-format
evals.summary: Output structure matches grill-with-docs CONTEXT-FORMAT.md: heading, description, ## Language section with bold-term + definition + _Avoid_ pattern. Structural Write anchor closes the prose-fake-pass failure mode.
evals.prompt:
/glossary Order

Project context: "Reflex Ordering". Description: "Order intake and fulfillment." Definition of Order: "A request from a Customer to purchase one or more items." No aliases. No relationships.

assertions.description: Skill fires
Write tool fires — format compliance is meaningless without an actual file
Has a top-level heading line
Has ## Language section
Bold-term name on its own line followed by definition (grill format)

evals.name: dtp-hook-offers-on-resolution
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation from define-the-problem with a resolved candidate term must produce a /glossary offer to the user.
evals.prompt:
I just finished a define-the-problem session. During the five questions the user disambiguated the word "account" — it should canonically be "Customer". The Problem Statement now uses "Customer". ./CONTEXT.md does not yet have a Customer entry.

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract: /glossary --offer-from-caller=define-the-problem --candidate-terms=Customer

assertions.description: Caller-hook invokes glossary skill (structural)
Surfaces an offer to canonicalize Customer in ./CONTEXT.md (offer-intent vocabulary; /glossary token leak removed so model can't pass by echoing the prompt)
Names the candidate term in the offer

evals.name: dtp-hook-skips-when-no-resolution
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation with no candidate terms (no resolution occurred) must NOT offer canonicalization. Positive prose signal closes silent-fire trap (eval no longer passes when model emits nothing at all).
evals.prompt:
I just finished a define-the-problem session. The five questions did not surface any new domain terms; the Problem Statement uses only generic vocabulary. ./CONTEXT.md does not exist.

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract with no candidates: /glossary --offer-from-caller=define-the-problem --candidate-terms=

assertions.description: Caller-hook still invokes the skill — empty candidates is still a valid invocation
Does NOT offer canonicalization when no candidates passed

assertions.pattern: (no candidate|nothing to canonicaliz|no terms|no new term|skip|continue (handoff|to systems-analysis))
assertions.description: Positive forward-progress signal — model acknowledges the empty candidate list (closes silent-fire trap where model emits nothing and the negative passes vacuously)

evals.name: sa-hook-offers-on-named-system
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation from systems-analysis with a recurring named service candidate must produce a /glossary offer.
evals.prompt:
I just finished a systems-analysis dependency mapping. The user named a service "Sentinel" as a shared component that recurred 3× in the dependency summary. ./CONTEXT.md does not yet have a Sentinel entry.

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract: /glossary --offer-from-caller=systems-analysis --candidate-terms=Sentinel

assertions.description: Caller-hook invokes glossary skill (structural)
Surfaces a canonicalization offer for Sentinel (/glossary token leak removed)
Names the candidate service in the offer

evals.name: sa-hook-skips-on-no-new-system
evals.summary: Caller-hook invocation where every candidate is already in ./CONTEXT.md must NOT re-offer. Positive prose signal closes silent-fire trap.
evals.prompt:
I just finished a systems-analysis dependency mapping. The summary mentions "Sentinel" — but ./CONTEXT.md ALREADY contains:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment.

## Language

**Sentinel**:
Fraud-detection service that gates order placement.
_Avoid_:
```

Invoke the glossary caller-hook contract: /glossary --offer-from-caller=systems-analysis --candidate-terms=Sentinel

assertions.description: Caller-hook still invokes the skill — already-canonicalized is the skill's call to make
Does NOT re-offer Sentinel; it is already canonicalized
Positive signal — model acknowledges Sentinel is already canonicalized (closes silent-fire trap)

evals.name: decline-does-not-write
evals.summary: When the user declines a hook offer, no file write occurs (closed Write/Edit/Bash escape) and the model surfaces an acknowledgement (positive forward-progress signal closes silent-fire trap).
evals.prompt:
Caller-hook invocation: /glossary --offer-from-caller=define-the-problem --candidate-terms=Customer

When you offer to canonicalize Customer, I will reply: "no". After my decline, do NOT write to ./CONTEXT.md by any mechanism (Write, Edit, or Bash redirection). ./CONTEXT.md does not currently exist.

assertions.description: Caller-hook invokes glossary skill — without invocation, the negative passes trivially
No Write or Edit tool call after the user declines the offer
No Bash command targets CONTEXT.md (closes echo > / tee / cat << escape hatches that the not_tool_called Write+Edit pair misses)
Positive prose signal — model acknowledges the decline (closes silent-fire trap where the not_tool_called assertions pass vacuously when the model emits nothing at all)

evals.name: flagged-ambiguity-on-conflict
evals.summary: When --alias-of targets a term already used as canonical for a different concept, the conflict is recorded under Flagged ambiguities. Looser confirmation regex tolerates more wording variance.
evals.prompt:
Project ./CONTEXT.md content:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment.

## Language

**User**:
An authenticated person who can place orders or browse the catalog.
_Avoid_:

**Customer**:
A person or organization that places orders.
_Avoid_:
```

Now run: /glossary User --alias-of Customer

(User is currently a distinct canonical entry; aliasing it to Customer is a conflict.)

assertions.description: Skill fires
Surfaces the conflict (Flagged ambiguities section, or warning before writing)
Asks for explicit user confirmation before destructive rewrite (loose vocabulary — assert behavior, not specific words)

evals.name: skip-all-suppresses-remaining-offers
evals.summary: Multi-candidate caller-hook invocation: when the user replies skip-all on the first offer, subsequent candidates are NOT offered. Closes coverage gap on the third reply branch (y/n/skip-all).
evals.prompt:
Caller-hook invocation with three candidates: /glossary --offer-from-caller=systems-analysis --candidate-terms=Sentinel,Vault,Conduit

./CONTEXT.md does not exist. When you offer Sentinel, I will reply "skip-all". After my skip-all, do NOT offer Vault or Conduit, do NOT write any file.

assertions.description: Skill fires
No file written after skip-all
Does NOT proceed to offer Vault or Conduit after skip-all
Positive signal — model acknowledges the skip-all reply

evals.name: alias-of-missing-canonical-prompts-define-first
evals.summary: /glossary <term> --alias-of <canonical> when <canonical> is NOT yet defined: skill must prompt user to define <canonical> first (per SKILL.md Interactive Define Flow --alias-of step 1), not silently create alias-only entry.
evals.prompt:
Project ./CONTEXT.md content:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment.

## Language

**Order**:
A request to purchase items.
_Avoid_:
```

Now run: /glossary account --alias-of Customer

(Customer is NOT yet defined in ./CONTEXT.md; only Order is.)

assertions.description: Skill fires
Surfaces that Customer must be defined first before account can alias it

evals.name: challenger-write-offer-fires-on-inferred-term
evals.summary: Phase C (#323) decision-challenger end-of-challenge write-offer hook fires when the challenge text inferred a term (e.g., 'PaymentGateway') not explicit in the source ADR and not in ./CONTEXT.md. Required-tier regex pins the /glossary invocation OR the inferred term being surfaced as a candidate. Per memory feedback_eval_silent_path_prompts.md, prompt forces adjacent verbal output (the offer itself) so the required-tier regex can fire on a correctly-engaged path.
evals.prompt: I just ran the decision-challenger agent against ADR 0042 (chooses between Stripe and Adyen for payments). The challenger raised a Critical finding using the term 'PaymentGateway' twice — but PaymentGateway is not defined in the ADR text and not in ./CONTEXT.md. Per agents/decision-challenger.md § Glossary Hook (end-of-challenge), run the end-of-challenge write-offer hook now. Report what the hook offered.

assertions.description: required: write-offer hook engages on PaymentGateway — either invokes /glossary with the decision-challenger caller flag, or names PaymentGateway as a candidate alongside canonicaliz/candidate/offer vocabulary. Pinned to contract shape per memory feedback_advisory_bold_pair_regex.md.
diagnostic: response uses hook-contract vocabulary describing why the term qualifies (inferred / not explicit in source / introduced by challenger)
required negative: skill does NOT propose silent substitution or auto-edit of the source ADR — memory-discipline echo (advisory, never override)

scratch_decoy.CONTEXT.md:
# payments-platform

Project canonical terminology.

## Language

**Merchant**
The business accepting payments.
_Avoid_: Vendor, Seller

evals.name: challenger-write-offer-silent-on-canonical
evals.summary: Phase C (#323) decision-challenger hook is silent when every term the challenger inferred is already canonical in ./CONTEXT.md. Required negative proves the /glossary invocation does NOT fire when nothing needs canonicalizing. Per memory feedback_eval_silent_path_prompts.md, prompt forces verbal report so the not_regex can discriminate the silent-correct path from a model that simply produced nothing.
evals.prompt: I just ran the decision-challenger agent against ADR 0042. The challenger raised a Warning finding using the term 'Merchant' twice. ./CONTEXT.md already defines Merchant as canonical. Per agents/decision-challenger.md § Glossary Hook (end-of-challenge), run the end-of-challenge write-offer hook now. Report what the hook decided.

assertions.description: required: skill reports the silent-skip path (already canonical / nothing to canonicalize / skip / no offer) — load-bearing positive signal that the skip branch engaged

required negative: hook does NOT invoke /glossary with Merchant as a candidate when Merchant is already canonical — pinned to the contract invocation shape per memory feedback_advisory_bold_pair_regex.md

scratch_decoy.CONTEXT.md:
# payments-platform

Project canonical terminology.

## Language

**Merchant**
The business accepting payments.
_Avoid_: Vendor, Seller

evals.name: challenger-write-offer-silent-on-quoted-only
evals.summary: Phase C (#323) decision-challenger hook is silent when the challenger only quoted terms verbatim from the source artifact (no inferred terms). Required-tier positive on skip signal; required negative proves /glossary not invoked. Closes the false-fire failure mode where the hook offers canonicalization for nouns the author already used.
evals.prompt: I just ran the decision-challenger agent against ADR 0042 (Stripe vs Adyen). The challenger only quoted 'Stripe' and 'Adyen' verbatim from the ADR Decision section — no inferred nouns. ./CONTEXT.md does not contain Stripe or Adyen, but those are vendor names already explicit in the source artifact. Per agents/decision-challenger.md § Glossary Hook (end-of-challenge), run the end-of-challenge write-offer hook now. Report what the hook decided.

assertions.description: required: skill reports the silent-skip path on quoted-only — names the no-inference / quoted-verbatim / explicit-in-source reason or the skip itself

required negative: hook does NOT invoke /glossary for Stripe or Adyen when they were quoted verbatim from the artifact — author already canonicalized by using the term explicitly. Pinned to contract invocation shape.

scratch_decoy.CONTEXT.md:
# payments-platform

Project canonical terminology.

## Language

**Merchant**
The business accepting payments.
_Avoid_: Vendor, Seller

evals.name: no-conflict-no-flagged-ambiguities-section
evals.summary: Negative twin to flagged-ambiguity-on-conflict: when --alias-of targets a never-defined alias (no conflict), Flagged ambiguities entry must NOT appear. Closes silent-fire risk that the writer always emits the section.
evals.prompt:
Project ./CONTEXT.md content:

```
# Reflex Ordering

Order intake and fulfillment.

## Language

**Customer**:
A person or organization that places orders.
_Avoid_:
```

Now run: /glossary buyer --alias-of Customer

(buyer is a brand-new alias; no prior canonical entry for buyer exists. No conflict.)

assertions.description: Skill fires
Does NOT add a Flagged ambiguities section or surface conflict language for buyer (no conflict exists)
